x-enumeration-literals.name: Lead Underwriter
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Main party that is awarded the mandate by a borrower to raise money via a new issue. The lead manager guarantees the liquidity of the deal, arranges the syndication of the issue and undertakes a major underwriting and distribution commitment. Also called Lead Manager. (ISO20022)

Comment:
Coordinates syndication and allocation.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Underwriter
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The party that forms, together with the lead underwriter, a syndicate of co-lead managers, co-managers and underwriters in order to raise money for a borrower via a new issue. (ISO20022)

Comment:
Assumes the financial risk and distributes the securities.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Issuer
x-enumeration-literals.description: The person or organization seeking to raise funds (e.g., via securities, loans, or insurance) and offers them to investors or policyholders.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Syndicate Member
x-enumeration-literals.description: Other underwriters participating in the risk.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Investor
x-enumeration-literals.description: Individuals who ultimately purchase the securities from underwriters.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Compliance Advisor
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Parties that ensure the underwriting meets regulatory and contractual obligations.

Comment:
Example: Law firm advising on bond prospectus and underwriting agreement.
